Oracle Events
- Search Airtable Relocations table for Keiko Tanaka's relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Keiko Tanaka") to find record recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle -- Columbus, OH -> Seattle, WA, Mosaic Robotics, In Progress, Move Start Date 2026-04-15, Coordinator: Javier Morales. Special Requirements: Epilog Fusion Pro 48 industrial laser cutter (400 lbs, ~$35K), Class 3B CO2 laser tube (75W), DOT 49 CFR Part 173 hazmat placarding required, Swift must sign laser equipment liability waiver, $800 hazmat handling surcharge, 8-day lease gap with interim storage at Swift Seattle facility, $450 interim storage fee.
- Search Airtable Relocations table for Joaquin Reyes's relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Joaquin Reyes") to find record recJoaquinReyesPhoenixSeattle -- Phoenix, AZ -> Seattle, WA, GreenStack Energy, In Progress, Move Start Date 2026-04-11, Move End Date 2026-04-19, Coordinator: Javier Morales. Special Requirements: 900-lb rooftop solar panel testing rig (8ft x 4ft x 3ft), delivery at GreenStack Seattle HQ (401 Terry Ave N, Suite 200), CRITICAL SCHEDULING CONFLICT: full building shutdown April 14-18 -- no building access, no freight elevator, no receiving dock. Rig delivery must be completed by EOD Sunday, April 13 OR be scheduled for Saturday, April 19, at 10:00 AM or later. Invoice deadline: April 25.
- Search Airtable Relocations table for Diego Castaneda's relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Diego Castaneda") to find record recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin -- Brooklyn, NY -> Austin, TX, PivotPoint Labs, In Progress, Move Start Date 2026-04-12, Move End Date 2026-05-02, Coordinator: Javier Morales. Special Requirements: Tormach PCNC 440 CNC mill (~600 lbs, $42,000 insured), vibration-dampened custom crating, climate-controlled transport (60-80°F), 16-day interim storage in Austin, cargo insurance minimum $50K. Critically: NO DOT hazmat classification -- CNC mill is heavy industrial equipment but does not require DOT documentation.
- Search Linear for the DOT compliance audit project using linear_list_projects (query: "DOT" or "compliance") to find project_dot_compliance_audit -- "DOT Compliance Audit -- Q1/Q2 2026 Hazmat Shipments", state: started, team: team_operations, lead: moveops_chloe_vance. Project description notes four moves in scope, three with complete documentation, one (Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter) with a missing placarding certificate requiring urgent remediation.
- List all issues in the DOT compliance audit project using linear_list_issues (project: "project_dot_compliance_audit") to find 5 issues: (1) issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack / "DOT Docs -- Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack / Class 9 Lithium", state: done; (2) issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic / "DOT Docs -- Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser", state: in_progress; (3) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind / "DOT Docs -- Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS", state: done; (4) issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack / "DOT Docs -- Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack / Oversize Rig", state: done (not hazardous material under 49 CFR Parts 171-180, flagged for overweight/oversize transport permits only); (5) issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert / "Obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift Relocations -- URGENT", state: todo. This reveals Keiko and Joaquin are in the DOT scope (Diego is not), and only Keiko has a documentation gap.
- Search Linear for the Keiko Tanaka DOT issue using linear_list_issues (query: "Keiko Tanaka" or project: "project_dot_compliance_audit") to find issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic -- state: in_progress, priority: 1, assignee: moveops_chloe_vance. Description documents the missing placarding certificate gap: Blessing Okafor received only verbal confirmation from Swift's driver at the April 15 pickup but never obtained a signed document. Greg Pallone's confirmation (April 22) is NOT in this ticket -- it is discoverable via email (OE 7) and Slack #operations. Ticket remains in_progress; signed copy has not been received by MoveOps.
- Read comments on the Keiko Tanaka DOT ticket using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic") to confirm no comments exist -- no resolution updates have been posted to the parent ticket.
- Read comments on the Swift placarding certificate subtask using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert") to confirm no comments exist, and the subtask remains in state: todo. Note: Chloe's status update about Greg Pallone's confirmation (comment_chloe_swift_confirmation, April 22) was posted on issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack rather than this subtask. Key conclusion: Swift confirmed the cert exists in their archive, but MoveOps has not received the signed copy as of April 26.
- Search emails for correspondence about the Swift placarding certificate using search_emails (query: "placarding" or "Tanaka Swift" or "Greg Pallone") to find email thread(s) between MoveOps and Swift about Keiko Tanaka's hazmat shipment. No email containing a signed placarding certificate attachment -- confirming the document remains outstanding as of April 26, 2026.
- Look up Greg Pallone's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Greg Pallone") to find greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com, Swift Relocations, National Account Manager.
- Add a comment to the existing Keiko Tanaka DOT ticket using linear_create_comment (issueId: "issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic", body: status update noting that as of April 26 the signed placarding certificate from Swift has not been received, a follow-up email has been sent to Greg Pallone, and this remains the sole documentation gap blocking the DOT compliance audit ahead of the June 5 deadline).
- Send email from Javier to Greg Pallone at Swift requesting the signed placarding certificate using send_email (sender: "javier.morales@moveops.com", recipients: ["greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com"], subject: referencing Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate / DOT compliance, content: requesting the signed DOT hazmat placarding certificate for Keiko Tanaka's Epilog laser cutter shipment from Columbus to Seattle, referencing the June 5, 2026 compliance deadline).
- Post a consolidated status update in #operations using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006", payload: covering all three equipment moves -- (a) Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic: DOT-regulated Class 3B laser, placarding certificate still missing from Swift, follow-up email sent to Greg Pallone, ticket updated; (b) Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack: oversize rig in DOT scope, documentation complete, but GreenStack Seattle building shutdown April 14-18 creates delivery scheduling conflict with current move window April 11-19; (c) Diego Castaneda / PivotPoint: CNC mill is heavy equipment but not DOT-regulated, no hazmat placarding required. Flagging Keiko's missing cert and Joaquin's delivery window as the two active blockers.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

2 The comment body (visible in the linear_create_comment body parameter) on the Keiko Tanaka DOT ticket states that the signed placarding certificate from Swift Relocations has not been received or is still outstanding (or similar).
outcome

Greg Pallone at Swift confirmed on ~April 23 that the cert exists...As of April 26, the signed certificate has not been received in our systems.' This clearly identifies what is still missing.
✓Run #2The comment body on issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic explicitly states: '(B) Hazmat placarding certificate — ❌ STILL MISSING (confirmed to exist at Swift, not yet received)' and references Swift and the outstanding signed certificate.

The criterion requires evidence in the `linear_create_comment` body parameter, which was never called. The update description does mention the outstanding placarding certificate, but it was placed in an issue description overwrite, not a comment.
✓Run #4The comment body on issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic explicitly states '(B) Hazmat placarding certificate — ❌ STILL MISSING (confirmed to exist at Swift but not yet in our possession)' and notes 'signed documents have NOT been received by MoveOps.' The missing document is clearly identified.
✓Run #5The comment body explicitly states 'The signed placarding certificate is **still not in hand** as of today' and lists '(B) Signed DOT hazmat placarding certificate — the single blocking item for the DOT response package. Greg confirmed it exists in Swift's compliance archive but has not delivered it.' Clearly identifies what is still missing.
✓Run #6The comment body explicitly states 'Placarding certificate status: STILL OUTSTANDING.' and mentions 'no follow-up email with the actual signed documents has been received,' clearly identifying the signed placarding certificate from Swift as the still-missing document.
3 The comment body (visible in the linear_create_comment body parameter) on the Keiko Tanaka DOT ticket references the June 5, 2026 compliance deadline or states this documentation gap must be resolved before June 5 (or similar deadline reference).
outcome
 ✓✓✗✓✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The comment body on issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic references the deadline explicitly: 'Follow-up email being sent to Greg today referencing the June 5 compliance deadline' and 'June 5 deadline: Marcus obtained a 15-day extension from PHMSA, moving the response deadline from May 21 to June 5.'
✓Run #2The comment body on issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic explicitly references 'The extended DOT compliance deadline is **June 5, 2026**' (per PHMSA Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187).

✓Run #5The comment body explicitly references 'the **June 5 compliance deadline**': 'I am emailing Greg Pallone directly from my account to escalate this request, referencing the **June 5 compliance deadline**.'
✓Run #6The comment body says 'referencing the June 5 extended compliance deadline,' directly satisfying the requirement to reference the June 5, 2026 deadline.
